hostCapability: kern.hv_support=1 on hw.model Mac14,7 (Apple M2), native arm64 with sysctl.proc_translated=0, so Virtualization.framework VM creation is supported on this host
hypothesis: The earlier VZErrorDomain Code=2 blocker was sandbox-denied Hypervisor access, not missing hardware support: the same signed binary on the same host created and booted the project LinuxPod when the up command ran outside the sandboxed shell, and the sandboxed context of the failed attempt is corroborated by sysmond being unavailable to pgrep during its cleanup. The remaining blocker is that the postgres:16-alpine DB managed process exits with status 1 immediately even with OCI image process defaults applied, and the LinuxPod executor does not capture service container stdout or stderr, so the postgres startup error is not yet observable. Service-level log capture is required to diagnose the DB exit.
observedRuntimeSequence: signed binary had com.apple.security.virtualization entitlement and doctor reported it present
escalated unsandboxed execution created the project runtime: both public images were pulled and rootfs ext4 images were built
project LinuxPod booted and vminitd served gRPC on vsock
